summary: Dan Shapiro shares a link to Lars Liden's walk-in closet renovation project, which included building an outdoor hutch for a hot water heater. While noting it's more home improvement than woodworking, Shapiro highlights the detailed photo documentation. He adds a practical tip about using shop-made shelf pin hole jigs for projects with movable shelves, linking to two resources.
key_points: Lars Liden's closet renovation included building an outdoor hutch for the hot water heater
The project is well-documented with detailed photos on Flickr
Shop-made shelf pin hole jigs save significant effort when building movable shelves
money_quotes: It's more a home-improvement than a woodworking project, but it's still worthy of note because he has detailed pictures of the whole thing.
If you build a project with movable shelves, instead of painstakingly locating each shelf pin hole, use a shop-made shelf pin hole jig.
key_insight: A shop-made shelf pin hole jig is a simple tool that eliminates tedious measurement work when building movable shelves.
